feed.title: Appu's blog
feed.subtitle: Somewhere where I will post some articles about things that matter to me

entry.title: The Mysterium dVPN
entry.summary: In the last days I set up a node of a decentralized VPN using the Mysterium network. This was mainly to get profits from the Internet bandwidth I don’t use and to help provide freedom of speech for those who need it. What is Mysterium? Mysterium is ‘a global, distributed network powered by everyday people. Network participants are incentivised to share their resources in a supply and demand...

entry.title: Protecting your server
entry.summary: I recently watched a NetworkChuck’s video where he explained how to protect your linux server (Dedicated to Ubuntu, but I plan on covering more than just that distro), so I thought that I could do some sort of writeup of the video as well as extend the info that is shown in it. Securing your linux server is an essential aspect to keep it running and to avoid any infiltrations in your network...

entry.title: Endlessh: SSH Honeypot
entry.summary: What’s this Endlessh is a piece of software that sets up a ssh honeypot for all the random people that may try to attack you by trying common username and password combinations to see if they can get access. In return, this program slows their access down hugely, and in a way someone with a script to do the attack won’t notice (most people doing these kind of attacks will be using scripts). ...

entry.title: My blog primer
entry.summary: The first post It is always difficult to start something new: new habits, a new job or maybe a new relationship. But I hope that this blog I’m deciding to start writing on from today once becomes a reference page for someone’s projects, inspiration or entertainment. I hope this becomes useful for someone that just wants to get answers that happen to be here, quickly. I don’t have many things ...
